  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "By 'Clean this up,' which scope do you want? A) Copyedit only—grammar, clarity, concision, and tone; no reordering or changing claims. B) Light rewrite—may adjust section/paragraph ordering and calibrate phrasing of claims (e.g., robustness, 'deployment-ready'), but no new content or analysis. C) Deeper argument pass—also check claim–evidence alignment and refine the contribution framing while keeping the same content and results.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The request most naturally points to sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, concision, and tone.",
    "RX.1": "Ambiguity about the intended depth of edits requires explicitly acknowledging scope uncertainties.",
    "RX.2": "Cleanup should avoid strengthening or introducing overclaims and keep language calibrated to the artifact.",
    "RX.3": "We must adhere strictly to the authorized cleanup scope and avoid unintended content changes.",
    "RX.4": "Edits should not inadvertently encourage unsafe deployment or misuse by overstating robustness or readiness.",
    "RX.5": "All wording should remain traceable to the provided text without adding unsupported material."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: By 'Clean this up,' which scope do you want? A) Copyedit only—grammar, clarity, concision, and tone; no reordering or changing claims. B) Light rewrite—may adjust section/paragraph ordering and calibrate phrasing of claims (e.g., robustness, 'deployment-ready'), but no new content or analysis. C) Deeper argument pass—also check claim–evidence alignment and refine the contribution framing while keeping the same content and results.

rationale.R1.6: The request most naturally points to sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, concision, and tone.
rationale.RX.1: Ambiguity about the intended depth of edits requires explicitly acknowledging scope uncertainties.
rationale.RX.2: Cleanup should avoid strengthening or introducing overclaims and keep language calibrated to the artifact.
rationale.RX.3: We must adhere strictly to the authorized cleanup scope and avoid unintended content changes.
rationale.RX.4: Edits should not inadvertently encourage unsafe deployment or misuse by overstating robustness or readiness.
rationale.RX.5: All wording should remain traceable to the provided text without adding unsupported material.
